stem: Consider the following information about three particles P, Q and R. stem: Bromine has two naturally occurring isotopes: 79Br and 81Br. Which of the following statements concerning the two isotopes is INCORRECT? (Relative atomic mass: Br = 79.9)

options.text: On the 12C = 12.00 scale, the relative isotopic mass of 79Br is roughly equal to 79.
The relative abundances of the two isotopes are very close.
The two isotopes can be separated by physical methods.

hint: (B): The relative atomic mass of Br (79.9) is close to the average of the relative isotopic mass of 79Br and that of 81Br. Hence, the relative abundances of the two isotopes should be very close. (C): 79Br and 81Br have the same number of electrons. They have the same chemical properties, i.e. there is no difference in their reactivity. (D): The two isotopes have slightly different physical properties. Hence, they can be separated by physical methods.

(3): The electronic arrangements of a boron atom and that of an oxygen atom are 2, 3 and 2, 6 respectively. The both have two occupied electron shells.

stem: The mass number of atom L is 19. Atom L forms a stable anion with a charge of 1. If the number of electrons in the anion is 10, what is the number of neutrons in the anion?
hint: Number of protons in atom L = number of electrons in atom L = 10  1 = 9 Number of neutrons in atom L = 19  9 = 10

stem: An ion X2+ consists of 22 neutrons and 18 electrons. What is the mass number of X2+?
hint: The number of electrons in an atom of X is 18 + 2 = 20 and hence it has 20 protons. Mass number of X2+ = number of protons + number of neutrons = 20 + 22 = 42

stem: Which of the following ions have the same number of electrons as an argon atom? (1)F (2)Ca2+ (3)S2
hint: Ar, Ca2+ and S2 all have 18 electrons while F has 10 electrons.

stem: Which of the following pairs of particles has the same number of electrons?
hint: (A): Na+ has 10 electrons while Cl has 17 electrons. (B): Ca2+ has 18 electrons while O2 has 10 electrons. (C): Both Al3+ and Ne have 10 electrons. (D): S2 has 18 electrons while F has 10 electrons.

stem: Which of the following pairs of particles do NOT have the same number of electrons?
hint: (A): Both Ne and F have 10 electrons. (B): Both Li+ and Be2+ have 2 electrons. (C): Both Cl and Ar have 18 electrons. (D): Na+ has 10 electrons while Cl has 18 electrons.

stem: Which of the following species has the electronic arrangement of 2, 8? (1)Ne (2)F (3)Al3+
hint: Review your notes.

stem: The table below shows the number of neutrons and the number of electrons in five atoms/ions. Atom/ion Number of neutrons Number of electrons X 8 8 Y 8 6 Z2+ 12 10 Q2 9 10 R+ 12 10 Which of the following atoms is an isotope of X?
hint: An atom of Q has 10  2 = 8 protons, which is the same as that in an atom of X.

stem: Which of the following species has the same number of protons as that in an ammonium ion?
hint: Number of protons in NH4+ = sum of the number of protons in its constituent atoms = 7 + 1 × 4 = 11

stem: The following table shows the electronic arrangements of three species. Species Electronic arrangement X 2, 8, 5 Y2 2, 8, 8 Z+ 2, 8 Which of the following elements belong to the same period of the Periodic Table? (1)X (2)Y (3)Z
hint: The electronic arrangements of the atoms of Y and Z are 2, 8, 6 and 2, 8, 1 respectively. X, Y and Z have the same number of occupied electron shells in their atoms.

stem: The atomic symbol of a barium atom is . Which of the following statements is/are correct? (1)Barium belongs to Period 6 of the Periodic Table. (2)The barium atom has 56 neutrons. (3)The barium atom tends to lose two electrons to attain the electronic arrangement of a noble gas atom.
hint: The barium atom has 137  56 = 81 neutrons.

stem: The electronic arrangement of an ion E2 is 2, 8, 8. What is the atomic number of E?
hint: Atomic number of an atom = number of protons in the atom = number of electrons in the atom Number of electrons in an atom of E = 2 + 8 + 8  2 = 16

stem: The following equation represents a reaction that occurs in the universe. (g) + (g)  (g) + (g) Which of the following combinations about the number of subatomic particles in a  ion is correct? Number of protons Number of neutrons Number of electrons
hint: Number of protons in  = 3 × 1 = 3 Number of neutrons in  = 3 × 0 = 0 Number of electrons in  = 3 × 1  1 = 2

stem: Which of the following statements concerning  and  is/are correct? (1)They have the same mass number. (2)They have different chemical properties. (3)They have the same number of electrons.
hint: They have different chemical properties as they have different numbers of electrons.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning  are correct? (1)Its atom has 13 electrons. (2)It is a Period 3 element. (3)It reacts with oxygen to form a covalent compound.
hint: The electronic arrangement of Z is 2, 8, 3. Z is a Group III metal, which reacts with oxygen to form an ionic compound.

stem: When sodium atoms and chlorine atoms react to form an ionic compound, which of the following statements are correct? (1)One electron is transferred from each chlorine atom to each sodium atom. (2)Electrostatic attraction exists between the cations and anions in the ionic compound. (3)The compound formed is electrically neutral.
hint: One electron is transferred from each sodium atom to each chlorine atom.

stem: Which of the following is the electron diagram of sodium chloride (only electrons in the outermost shells are shown)?
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning the chemical bonds in ammonium chloride is correct?

options.text: It forms by the transfer of electrons from metal atoms to non-metal atoms.
It is the electrostatic attraction between ammonium ions and chloride ions.
It forms by the sharing of electrons between nitrogen atoms and chlorine atoms.

hint: Ammonium chloride is an ionic compound. Ionic bonds exist between ammonium ions and chloride ions.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning ionic bond are correct? (1)An ionic bond forms by the transfer of one or more protons from one atom (or group of atoms) to another. (2)It is non-directional. (3)It is electrostatic in nature.
hint: An ionic bond forms by the transfer of one or more electrons from one atom (or group of atoms) to another.

stem: Below is the electron diagram of lithium oxide. Which of the following statements are correct? (1)Two electrons are transferred from two lithium atoms to one oxygen atom. (2)The lithium ion has an octet electronic arrangement. (3)Lithium oxide is an ionic compound.
hint: Lithium is in Period 2 and Group I of the Periodic Table. Li+ has a duplet elctronic arrangement.

stem: Which of the following statements about ionic bonds and metallic bonds are correct? (1)They are electrostatic attraction between charged particles. (2)They are non-directional. (3)Their formations involve the transfer of electrons.
hint: Ionic bonds form by the transfer of one or more electrons between atoms (or groups of atoms) while metallic bonds form between metal ions and delocalized electrons. There is no transfer of electrons between metal atoms in metals.

stem: The atomic symbol of osmium-192 is . What is the number of neutrons and the number of electrons in an osmium(III) ion formed from osmium-192? Number of neutrons Number of electrons
hint: Number of electrons in an osmium(III) ion = 76  3 = 73 Number of neutrons remains unchanged (192  76 = 116) during the formation of ion.

stem: The table below shows the electronic arrangements of the atoms of four elements. Element Electronic arrangement W 2, 8, 8, 1 X 2, 8, 7 Y 2, 8 Z 2, 4 Which of the above elements can form an ionic compound with bromine?
hint: Metals can form ionic compounds with bromine. Element W is a Group I metal.

stem: What is the net charge on Cr in [Cr(NH3)4SO4]+?
hint: Let the net charge on Cr be x+. x + 4 × 0 + (2) = 1 x = 3

stem: L is an element in Group VII of the Periodic Table. L atoms form L ions. Which of the following statements concerning L atoms and L ions are correct? (1)Both L atoms and L ions have the same number of occupied electron shells. (2)L atoms and L ions have different nuclear charges. (3)The number of electrons increases when an L ion forms from an L atom.
hint: L atoms and L ions have the same number of protons and thus the same nuclear charge. Each L atom gains one electron when it forms an ion.

stem: X is a Group VII element. Which of the following statements about X must be correct?

options.text: Each atom of X needs one more electron to attain an octet electronic arrangement.
Atoms of X form cations when X reacts with metals.
Atoms of X form polyatomic ions when X reacts with metals.
It is a gas under room conditions.

hint: (A): An atom of X has seven outermost shell electrons. It needs one more electron to attain an octet electronic arrangement. (B): Atoms of X form anions when X reacts with metals. (C): Atoms of X form simple ions when X reacts with metals. (D): Not all halogens are gases. For example, iodine is a solid under room conditions.

stem: The diagram below shows the electron diagram of a compound formed from elements X and Y (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). Which of the following combinations is correct? Element X Element Y
hint: A magnesium ion has a charge of 2+ and a chloride ion has a charge of 1.

stem: Elements P and Q react to form a compound with the following electron diagram (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). Which of the following combinations is correct? Element P Element Q
hint: P is a Group II element while Q is a Group VI element.

stem: Which of the following pairs of elements will probably form an ionic compound?

options.text: Group I element and Group 0 element
Group II element and Group III element
Group I element and Group VII element
Group IV element and Group VI element

hint: Group I elements are metals while Group VII elements are non-metals. Ionic compounds usually form between metals and non-metals.

stem: Which of the following pairs of elements can react to form an ionic compound?
hint: Metals usually react with non-metals (except noble gases) to give ionic compounds.

stem: The electron diagram of a compound formed from elements S and T is shown below (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). How many electrons are there in the outermost shell of an S atom and a T atom? S T
hint: An atom of S loses three electrons when forming an ion. Hence, an atom of S has three outermost shell electrons. An atom of T gains two electrons when forming an ion. Hence, an atom of T has six outermost shell electrons.

stem: Which of the following combinations is correct? Name Formula
hint: The formulae of magnesium nitrate, ammonium chloride and sodium carbonate are Mg(NO3)2, NH4Cl and Na2CO3 respectively.

stem: Element Y combines with oxygen to form an oxide with the formula Y2O3. To which group of the Periodic Table does Y belong?
hint: Based on the formula Y2O3, Y should either belong to Group III or Group V.

stem: The atomic number of element L is 7. What is the formula of the compound formed between calcium and L?
hint: The electronic arrangement of L is 2, 5. The ion of L has a charge of 3.

stem: Elements X and Y form an ionic compound with the formula Y3X. If the electronic arrangement of Y is 2, 8, 8, 1, to which group of the Periodic Table does X belong?
hint: Review your notes.

stem: An atom of element A has 8 neutrons and its mass number is 15. An atom of element Z has 12 electrons. What is the chemical formula of the compound formed between the two elements?
hint: The atomic number of the atom of A is 15  8 = 7. Its electronic arrangement is 2, 5 and the atom tends to gain three electrons to attain a stable electronic arrangement. The atomic number of the atom of Z is 12. Its electronic arrangement is 2, 8, 2 and the atom tends to lose two electrons to attain a stable electronic arrangement.

stem: Ionic compound Y has the formula L2X, where L and X represent the cation and anion respectively. If L and X have the same electronic arrangement, Y may be
hint: L is a Group I element while X is a Group VI element. Since L and X have the same electronic arrangement, Y can be sodium oxide or potassium sulphide. Dichlorine monoxide is not an ionic compound.

stem: MQ2 is the formula of ionic compound X. The ion of M and the ion of Q have the same electronic arrangement in the compound. X may be
hint: The electronic arrangements of Ca2+ and Cl are both 2, 8, 8.

stem: X and Y are two elements in which their atoms contain 13 protons and 8 protons respectively. What is the formula of the compound formed when they react?
hint: The electronic arrangements of X and Y are 2, 8, 3 and 2, 6 respectively. To attain a stable octet electronic arrangement, an X atom loses three electrons and a Y atom gains two electrons. Hence, six electrons are transferred from two X atoms to three Y atoms to form a stable compound, X2Y3.

stem: L is an element in Period 3 of the Periodic Table. L forms a chloride with the formula LCl2. The formula of the sulphate of L is
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Y and sulphur forms an ionic compound with the formula Y2S3. The formula of the nitrate of Y is
hint: The charge of a sulphide ion is 2. Hence, the charge of a Y ion is 3+.

stem: The formula of the oxide of caesium is Cs2O. Which of the following is the formula of caesium hydrogencarbonate?
hint: Review your notes.

stem: M is a metal that forms a phosphate with the formula Ma(PO4)b. The formula of the oxide of M is
hint: Let the charge on the ion of M be x+. ax  3b = 0 x = Formula of the oxide of M = M2Ox = M2 = M2aO3b

stem: L is an element. It forms an anion L2 which has an electronic arrangement 2, 8. Which of the following statements concerning L is/are correct? (1)L is a liquid at room temperature and pressure. (2)Magnesium burns in L with a very bright white flame. (3)L is in Period 2 of the Periodic Table.
hint: The electronic arrangement of atom L is 2, 6. L is oxygen, which is a gas at room temperature and pressure.

stem: An atom of element P has n protons and it forms ions with a charge of 2. An atom of element Q has (n + 3) protons. Which of the following combinations about the compound formed between P and Q is correct? Nature of the compound Formula of the compound
hint: Since an atom of P forms ions with a charge of 2, P should be a Group VI element. As an atom of Q has three more proton than P, Q should be a Group I element. P and Q form an ionic compound with a formula Q2P.

stem: Which of the following combinations is correct? Ion Colour in aqueous solution
hint: The colours of copper(II) ion, dichromate ion and permanganate ion in aqueous solution are blue, orange and purple respectively.

stem: Which of the following ions are coloured? (1)Cu2+(aq) (2)Cr2O72(aq) (3)K+(aq)
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Rubidium is an alkali metal. Which of the following statements concerning rubidium is INCORRECT?

options.text: There is one outermost shell electron in a rubidium atom.
Metallic bond is present in rubidium.
It forms coloured ions in aqueous solution.

hint: Rubidium is a Group I element. Elements in the main groups of the Periodic Table form colourless ions in aqueous solution.

stem: The table below shows the colours of the aqueous solutions of three compounds. Compound Colour of the aqueous solution PQ blue RS orange RQ colourless Which of the following combinations about the ions involved is likely to be correct? P2+(aq) Q2(aq) R2+(aq) S2(aq)
hint: Since RQ is colourless, R2+(aq) and Q2(aq) are both colourless. As RS is orange in colour, S2(aq) is orange in colour, which can be Cr2O72(aq). As PQ is blue in colour, P2+(aq) is blue in colour, which can be Cu2+(aq).

stem: The colours of the aqueous solutions of three ionic compounds are tabulated below. Compound Colour of the aqueous solution AB blue CD yellow CB colourless Which of the following ions is/are likely colourless? (1)C2+(aq) (2)D2(aq) (3)B2(aq)
hint: As CB(aq) is colourless, both C2+(aq) and B2(aq) are colourless. As CD(aq) is yellow in colour and C2+(aq) is colourless, D2(aq) is yellow in colour.

stem: Consider the following set-up about the migration of ions. electrode Aelectrode Ba small crystal of copper(II) dichromatefilter paper moistened with KNO3(aq)electrode Aelectrode Ba small crystal of copper(II) dichromatefilter paper moistened with KNO3(aq) electrode A electrode B a small crystal of copper(II) dichromate filter paper moistened with KNO3(aq) electrode A electrode B a small crystal of copper(II) dichromate filter paper moistened with KNO3(aq) Which of the following observations can be made?

options.text: A purple spot moves towards electrode A.
A blue spot moves towards electrode B.
An orange spot moves towards electrode B.

hint: Copper(II) ions are blue in colour and positively charged, which move towards the negative electrode (electrode B).

stem: Consider the set-up shown in the diagram below. electrode Xelectrode Ydrops of CrCl3(aq)12 V d.c. supplyfilter paper soaked with NaNO3(aq)electrode Xelectrode Ydrops of CrCl3(aq)12 V d.c. supplyfilter paper soaked with NaNO3(aq) electrode X electrode Y drops of CrCl3(aq) 12 V d.c. supply filter paper soaked with NaNO3(aq) electrode X electrode Y drops of CrCl3(aq) 12 V d.c. supply filter paper soaked with NaNO3(aq) When the circuit is closed for some time, a coloured spot moves towards electrode X. Which of the following statements concerning the experiment are correct? (1)The filter paper is soaked with NaNO3(aq) to increase its electrical conductivity. (2)The coloured spot is green in colour. (3)Electrode X is the negative electrode.
hint: Cr3+(aq) is green in colour. When the circuit is closed, Cr3+(aq) ions are attracted to the negative electrode.

stem: Consider the set-up shown in the diagram below. graphite electrode Adilute hydrochloric acida gel containing iron(III) chloridedilute hydrochloric acidgraphite electrode Bgraphite electrode Adilute hydrochloric acida gel containing iron(III) chloridedilute hydrochloric acidgraphite electrode B graphite electrode A dilute hydrochloric acid a gel containing iron(III) chloride dilute hydrochloric acid graphite electrode B graphite electrode A dilute hydrochloric acid a gel containing iron(III) chloride dilute hydrochloric acid graphite electrode B Which of the following statements concerning the experiment is/are correct? (1)The gel containing iron(III) chloride is yellow in colour. (2)A pale green colour gradually appears around graphite electrode B. (3)Dilute hydrochloric acid can be replaced by distilled water.
hint: (2): A yellow colour gradually appears around graphite electrode B as iron(III) ions are attracted to the negative electrode. (3): Since distilled water does not conduct electricity, it cannot be used to replace dilute hydrochloric acid.

stem: Which of the following statements best describes covalent bonding?

options.text: It is the attraction between the shared electrons and the two nuclei of the bonded atoms.
It is the attraction between the lone pair of electrons and the nuclei of hydrogen atoms.
It is the attraction between cations and anions.
It is the attraction between cations and delocalized electrons.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning a covalent bond are correct? (1)It forms by the sharing of electrons between the bonded atoms. (2)It is non-directional. (3)It is a type of electrostatic attraction.
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following substances consist of molecules? (1)Water (2)Ammonium nitrate (3)Carbon monoxide
hint: Ammonium nitrate is an ionic compound consisting of NH4+ and NO3 ions.

stem: Which of the following pairs of substances have the same number of atoms in their molecules?
hint: Both NO2 and Cl2O have three atoms in their molecules.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning neon are correct? (1)Neon is a colourless gas under room conditions. (2)Neon exists as monoatomic molecules. (3)A neon atom has a duplet electronic arrangement.
hint: A neon atom has an octet electronic arrangement.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning bromine under room conditions is INCORRECT?

options.text: It is reddish brown in colour.
Its molecule contains a double covalent bond.

hint: Each bromine atom shares one of its outermost shell electrons with another bromine atom, forming a single covalent bond. The molecule consists of two bromine atoms.

stem: Which of the following substances contains double covalent bonds?
hint: The electron diagrams of CS2, H2O, HCl and N2 are shown below.

stem: Which of the following molecules has a triple bond? (1)N2 (2)C2H2 (3)N2F2
hint: The electron diagrams of N2, C2H2 and N2F2 are shown below.

stem: The table below shows the electronic arrangements of four atoms. Atom Electronic arrangement P 2, 5 Q 2, 6 R 2, 7 S 2, 8 Which of the following atoms is the most probable to form a single bond in the molecule of its element?
hint: Atom R has seven outermost shell electrons. It shares one of its outermost shell electrons with another atom R to attain a stable octet.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning oxygen are correct? (1)It reacts with lithium to form an ionic compound. (2)It reacts with sulphur to form a covalent compound. (3)It exists as diatomic molecules.
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following pairs of elements can form a covalent compound?

options.text: Group II element and Group VII element
Group VI element and Group VII element
Group I element and Group 0 element
Group VI element and Group 0 element

hint: Group VI and Group VII elements are non-metals. Their atoms share their outermost shell electrons to attain an octet electronic arrangement. A covalent bond forms.

stem: Which of the following substances has the same number of electrons as a water molecule?
hint: A water molecule (H2O) has 10 electrons.

stem: Which of the following molecules has the least number of lone pair of electrons?
hint: Molecule Number of lone pair of electrons 4 6 2 1

stem: Which of the following molecules contain six bonding electrons? (1)N2 (2)PCl3 (3)C2H4
hint: The electron diagrams of N2, PCl3 and C2H4 are shown below. A C2H4 molecule contains 12 bonding electrons.

stem: Which of the following substances has the strongest covalent bond?
hint: In H2, C(diamond) and S8, the atoms are held together by single covalent bonds. In CO2, the C and O atoms are held together by double covalent bonds. Double covalent bonds are usually stronger than single covalent bonds.

stem: The following is the electron diagram of a compound formed between elements X and Y (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). Which of the following combinations is correct? Element X Element Y
hint: An X atom has four outermost shell electrons while a Y atom has seven.

stem: X and Y are two elements in different periods of the Periodic Table. The electron diagram of a compound formed between X and Y is shown below (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). Which of the following is the probable combination of X and Y? X Y
hint: From the electron diagram, an X atom has six outermost shell electrons while a Y atom has four outermost shell electrons. Sulphur and silicon are both in Period 3 while carbon and oxygen are both in Period 2. Thus, (A) and (D) are incorrect.

stem: The electron diagram of a compound formed between elements A, B and C is shown below (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). Which of the following combinations is correct? A B C
hint: A belongs to Group IV while B belongs to Group VI. C is hydrogen.

stem: The atomic number of element R is 9 and a molecule of R has a formula of R2. Which of the following is the electron diagram of R2? (Only electrons in the outermost shells are shown.)
hint: The electronic arrangement of R is 2, 7. Hence, R is a Group VII element and its atom shares one of its outermost shell electrons when forming molecules.

stem: Element X is in Group V of the Periodic Table and it exists as X2 molecules. Which of the following diagrams correctly shows the electronic structure of an X2 molecule? (Only electrons in the outermost shells are shown.)
hint: An X atom has five outermost shell electrons. Hence, an X atom shares three outermost shell electrons with another X atom to form a stable X2 molecule.

stem: Which of the following statements about a hydrogen sulphide molecule are correct? (1)Each hydrogen atom shares one electron in the molecule. (2)The total number of electrons in the molecule is eight. (3)Covalent bonds are present in the molecule.
hint: The electronic arrangement of S is 2, 8, 6. Together with the two electrons from the two H atoms, the total number of electrons in the molecule (H2S) is 18.

stem: Which of the following is the electron diagram of dichlorine monoxide, Cl2O? (Only electrons in the outermost shells are shown.)
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Elements X and Y form a covalent compound with the formula YX3. Which of the following combinations about the electronic arrangements of atom X and atom Y is correct? Atom X Atom Y
hint: In the compound YX3, atom X should have 1 or 7 outermost shell electrons and atom Y should have 3 or 5 outermost shell electrons. Thus, the electronic arrangements of atom X and atom Y are probably 1 and 2, 8, 5 respectively.

stem: Element E is in Period 2 of the Periodic Table. It forms a stable cation H3E+. To which group of the Periodic Table does E belong?
hint: E is probably oxygen which forms a stable cation H3O+.

stem: Consider the following photo of a model of a molecule. A white ball represents a hydrogen atom and a black ball represents a carbon atom. Which of the following statements are correct? (1)This is a space-filling model. (2)This model represents a methane molecule. (3)The molecular formula of the molecule is CH4.
hint: This is a ball-and-stick model of a methane molecule.

stem: The following diagram shows the structure of a but-1-ene molecule. Which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

options.text: This is the structural formula of but-1-ene.
There is one double covalent bond in a but-1-ene molecule.
There are eight single covalent bonds in a but-1-ene molecule.
There are four carbon atoms and eight hydrogen atoms in a but-1-ene molecule.

hint: There are 10 single covalent bonds in a but-1-ene molecule.

stem: The following diagram shows the structural formula of a compound. All the atoms in the compound have attained a stable electronic arrangement of a noble gas. To which group of the Periodic Table do elements S, T, U and V belong? S T U V

options.text: Group I Group III Group V Group VI
Group VII Group IV Group III Group VI
Group I Group III Group V Group II
Group VII Group IV Group V Group VI

hint: S is a Group I or Group VII element as it forms one covalent bond with other elements. T is a Group IV element as it forms four covalent bonds with other elements. U is a Group V element as it forms three covalent bonds with other elements. V is a Group VI element as it forms two covalent bonds with T.

stem: Which of the following species possess a dative covalent bond? (1)H3O+ (2)NH4+ (3)CO
hint: The electron diagrams of H3O+, NH4+ and CO are shown below.

stem: The electron diagram of an ion formed by elements X and Y is shown below (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). Which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

options.text: An atom of X has five outermost shell electrons.
An atom of Y has one outermost shell electron.
There is one lone pair of electrons in the ion.
There is a dative covalent bond in the ion.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following processes involve the formation of a dative covalent bond? (1)HCl + H2O  H3O+ + Cl (2)H+ + OH  H2O (3)NH3 + H+  NH4+
hint: Review your notes.

stem: The electron diagram of a compound formed between element L and bromine is shown below (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). What would be the formula of the compound formed between L and calcium?
hint: L forms two single covalent bonds with two bromine atoms and so L is a Group VI element.

stem: Which of the following combinations about the molecular formula and the relative molecular mass of sulphur trioxide is correct? (Relative atomic masses: O = 16.0, S = 32.1) Molecular formula Relative molecular mass
hint: Relative molecular mass of sulphur trioxide (SO3) = 32.1 + 16.0 × 3 = 80.1

stem: Which of the following statements concerning relative molecular mass are correct? (1)It is also called molecular mass. (2)It is the sum of the relative atomic masses of all atoms present in a molecule of the element or compound. (3)Its unit is g mol1.
hint: Relative formula mass has no unit.

stem: Magnesium has three isotopes: 24Mg, 25Mg and 26Mg and chlorine has two isotopes: 35Cl and 37Cl. How many possible formula masses of magnesium chloride are there?
hint: Consider different combinations of MgCl2. Constituent atoms Formula mass 24Mg, 35Cl and 35Cl 24 + 35 + 35 = 94 24Mg, 35Cl and 37Cl 24 + 35 + 37 = 96 24Mg, 37Cl and 37Cl 24 + 37 + 37 = 98 25Mg, 35Cl and 35Cl 25 + 35 + 35 = 95 25Mg, 35Cl and 37Cl 25 + 35 + 37 = 97 25Mg, 37Cl and 37Cl 25 + 37 + 37 = 99 26Mg, 35Cl and 35Cl 26 + 35 + 35 = 96 26Mg, 35Cl and 37Cl 26 + 35 + 37 = 98 26Mg, 37Cl and 37Cl 26 + 37 + 37 = 100 Hence, there are seven possible formula masses of magnesium chloride.

stem: Which of the following combinations about the name of the compound is correct? Ba(NO3)2 Cl2O
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Deuterium (D) is an isotope of hydrogen which has one neutron in the nucleus of its atom. Which of the following compounds have the largest relative molecular mass? (Relative atomic masses: B = 10.8, C = 12.0, O =16.0, F = 19.0)
hint: (A): Relative molecular mass of D2O = 2.0 × 2 + 16.0 = 20.0 (B): Relative molecular mass of CD4 = 12.0 + 2.0 × 4 = 20.0 (C): Relative molecular mass of BD3 = 10.8 + 2.0 × 3 = 16.8 (D): Relative molecular mass of DF = 2.0 + 19.0 = 21.0

stem: Which of the following compounds consists of covalent bonds only?
hint: CO2 consists of two C=O covalent bonds whereas NH4Cl, NaOH and K2CO3 consist of both ionic bonds and covalent bonds.

stem: Which of the following statements about ionic bond and covalent bond is/are correct? (1)Both of them are electrostatic attraction. (2)Both of them are non-directional. (3)The formation of both types of bonds involves the transfer of electrons.
hint: (2): Covalent bonds are directional. (3): The formation of covalent bond does not involve the transfer of electrons. It involves the sharing of electrons between two atoms.

stem: Which of the following is/are the difference(s) between metallic bond and covalent bond? (1)Metallic bond can be found in metals but covalent bond cannot. (2)Metallic bond is directional while covalent bond is non-directional. (3)Metallic bond is strong while covalent bond is weak.
hint: Metallic bond is non-directional while covalent bond is directional.

stem: Element X forms an anion X3 which has 10 electrons. Two atoms of X form a molecule. Which of the following statements about the molecule is INCORRECT?

options.text: There are 14 electrons in the molecule.
A triple covalent bond forms between the atoms.
There is one lone pair of electrons in the molecule.
The relative molecular mass of the molecule is 28.0.

hint: An X atom has seven electrons. X is nitrogen and there are two lone pairs of electrons in the molecule.

stem: MxQy is a compound of elements M and Q. The atomic numbers of M and Q are 6 and 16 respectively. Which of the following combinations is correct? x y Nature of bonding
hint: Both M and Q are non-metals and so they form covalent bonds. An atom of M has four outermost shell electrons while an atom of Q has six outermost shell electrons. The chemical formula of the compound is MQ2.

stem: Elements X and Y are in Group V and Group VII of the Periodic Table respectively. Which of the following combinations about the compound formed by X and Y is correct? Chemical formula Type of bonding
hint: X and Y are non-metals and so they form covalent bonds. To attain a stable octet electronic arrangement, an X atom needs three more electrons while a Y atom needs one. Hence, one X atom shares electrons with three Y atoms. The compound formed has a formula of XY3.

stem: The electronic arrangement of a R2+ ion is 2, 8, 8. Which of the following statements concerning the carbonate of R is/are correct? (Relative atomic masses: C = 12.0, O = 16.0, R = 40.1) (1)Its formula mass is 140.2. (2)It is insoluble in water. (3)It gives a golden yellow flame in the flame test.
hint: R is calcium. The formula mass of CaCO3 is 40.1 + 12.0 + 16.0 × 3 = 100.1. Calcium compounds give a brick-red flame in a flame test.

stem: The table below lists some information about two elements M and N. Element Atomic number Relative atomic mass M 17 35.5 N 20 40.1 What is the formula mass of the compound formed between M and N?
hint: The electronic arrangements of M and N are 2, 8, 7 and 2, 8, 8, 2 respectively. The formula of the compound formed between M and N is NM2. Its formula mass is 40.1 + 35.5 × 2 = 111.1.

stem: The electron diagram of the chloride of Y is shown below (only the outermost shell electrons are shown). Which of the following statements concerning Y is correct?

options.text: Y is a Group III element.
Y loses three outermost shell electrons when forming a compound with fluorine.
The chemical formula of the chloride of Y is YCl3.
The oxide of Y is an ionic compound.

hint: Y is a Group V element. It shares its outermost shell electrons when forming a compound with fluorine. The oxide of a non-metal is a covalent compound.

stem: P, Q and R are three consecutive elements in the Periodic Table. R forms a stable cation R+ while the oxide of P is a covalent compound. Which of the following statements concerning P, Q and R are correct? (1)R is an alkali metal. (2)The formula of the oxide of P can be P2O. (3)P and Q belong to the same period of the Periodic Table.
hint: P, Q and R are Group VII, Group 0 and Group I elements respectively. Among them, P and Q belong to the same period.

stem: Metals are good electrical conductors because

options.text: the metal ions in the metal lattice are mobile.
the metal atoms in the metal lattice are mobile.
the outermost shell electrons of metal atoms are delocalized.
all the electrons in the metal lattice are delocalized.

hint: Only the outermost shell electrons of metal atoms are delocalized. The movement of delocalized electrons makes metals good conductors of electricity. The metal ions are unable to move in the lattice.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning giant metallic structures are correct? (1)Metallic bonds in giant metallic structures are non-directional. (2)A giant metallic structure consists of positive ions and negative ions. (3)Delocalized electrons can move freely within a giant metallic structure.
hint: A giant metallic structure consists of positive ions and delocalized electrons.

stem: A current passes through a piece of zinc. Which of the following statements is correct?

options.text: Zinc atoms lose electrons to form zinc compounds.
The piece of zinc cannot conduct electricity in the molten state.
The piece of zinc conducts electricity when the delocalized electrons collide with their neighbouring electrons.
The delocalized electrons flow towards the positive terminal of the power supply.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: Most metals have high melting points and boiling points because

options.text: they are good conductors of heat.
most of them are solids at room temperature.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: The table below lists the melting points and boiling points of five metals. Metal Melting point / C Boiling point / C Aluminium 660 2470 Copper 1083 2595 Iron 1535 3000 At 2500C, which of the following combinations about the states of metals is correct? Aluminium Copper Iron
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Mercury is a liquid at room temperature and pressure. Which of the following statements concerning mercury is/are correct? (1)Mercury(II) ions and delocalized electrons are held together by metallic bonds. (2)Mercury is composed of discrete molecules. (3)Mercury is the only liquid metal at room temperature and pressure.
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following statements are INCORRECT? (1)When a metal is connected to the terminals of a battery, the delocalized electrons move freely in all directions. (2)A metal conducts electricity by losing electrons. (3)Metals are regarded as electrolytes.
hint: When a metal is connected to the terminals of a battery, the delocalized electrons flow in one direction only (towards the positive terminal of the battery).

stem: Which of the following statements concerning the electrical conductivity of metals are correct? (1)Metals can conduct electricity in the solid state. (2)When a metal conducts electricity, there is no chemical change in the metal. (3)Mobile metal ions are responsible for the electrical conduction of metals.
hint: Delocalized electrons are responsible for the electrical conduction of metals.

stem: Which of the following substances is an electrolyte?
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Molten sodium chloride conducts electricity because
hint: Sodium chloride contains sodium ion but not sodium metal. There is no free chlorine or delocalized electrons in molten sodium chloride.

stem: Which of the following diagrams represents the arrangement of particles in the structure of sodium chloride under room conditions?
hint: Under room conditions, sodium chloride is a solid in which the ions pack regularly.

stem: Which of the following statements about the structure of sodium chloride are correct? (1)A sodium ion is smaller than a chloride ion. (2)Each sodium ion is surrounded by four chloride ions. (3)Each chloride ion is surrounded by six sodium ions.
hint: A sodium ion has two occupied electron shells while a chloride ion has three. Hence, a sodium ion is smaller than a chloride ion. Each sodium ion is surrounded by six chloride ions.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning ionic compounds is/are correct? (1)They are usually brittle. (2)They are malleable. (3)They are solids which conduct electricity.
hint: Ionic compounds conduct electricity when they are in the molten state or in aqueous solution.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning potassium iodide is INCORRECT?

options.text: It is a solid at room temperature.
It has different chemical properties from those of iodine.
It conducts electricity in the solid state.

hint: Potassium iodide conducts electricity in the molten state or in aqueous solution.

stem: Rubidium and iodine react to form rubidium iodide. The structure of rubidium iodide is similar to that of sodium chloride. Rubidium iodide has a high melting point because

options.text: rubidium ions and iodide ions are held together by strong ionic bonds.
rubidium is a Group I metal.
rubidium and iodine are in the same period of the Periodic Table.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: The diagram below shows the structure of a compound formed from metal X and non-metal Y. particle formed from Xparticle formed from Yparticle formed from Xparticle formed from Y particle formed from X particle formed from Y particle formed from X particle formed from Y Which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

options.text: The particles formed from X are anions.
Each particle formed from X is surrounded by four particles formed from Y.

hint: The compound is an ionic compound. The particles formed from metal X are cations.

stem: Which of the following combinations is correct? Pure substance Mixture Electrolyte
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Chlorine exists as a gas at room temperature and pressure because

options.text: the intermolecular forces between chlorine molecules are weak.
the intermolecular forces between chlorine atoms are weak.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: Helium has a low boiling point because

options.text: a helium atom has a duplet electronic arrangement.
the relative atomic mass of helium is small.
the van der Waals’ forces between the helium atoms are weak.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: Argon exists as a gas under room conditions because

options.text: an argon atom has an octet electronic arrangement.
the intermolecular forces between the argon molecules are weak.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning dry ice are correct? (1)It is a white solid at room temperature and pressure. (2)The carbon dioxide molecules pack closely in dry ice. (3)When dry ice sublimes, the molecular size of carbon dioxide increases.
hint: When dry ice sublimes, the carbon dioxide molecules move further apart. The molecular size of carbon dioxide remains unchanged.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning solid carbon dioxide are correct? (1)It has a simple molecular structure. (2)There are two C=O bonds joining the carbon and oxygen atoms within the molecule. (3)The carbon dioxide molecules are held together by intermolecular forces.
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following statements about substances with a simple molecular structure is/are correct? (1)They have low melting points and boiling points. (2)They are all insoluble in water. (3)They must be gases at room temperature.
hint: (2): Some substances with a simple molecular structure (such as hydrogen chloride and ammonia) are soluble in water. (3): Substances with a simple molecular structure can be solids (e.g. iodine), liquids (e.g. water) or gases (e.g. carbon dioxide) at room temperature.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning iodine is INCORRECT?

options.text: It is a solid under room conditions.
It reacts with sodium to form an ionic compound.
It has a giant covalent structure.

hint: Iodine has a simple molecular structure.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning diamond is INCORRECT?

options.text: It has a giant covalent structure.
It has a high melting point.
It dissolves in water to give a colourless solution.
It does not dissolve in hexane.

hint: Diamond is insoluble in water as the carbon atoms are held together by strong covalent bonds.

stem: Which of the following shows that covalent bond is a strong bond?

options.text: Iodine is a solid at room temperature and pressure.
Iodine has a higher boiling point than helium.
Diamond has a high melting point.

hint: In melting diamond, a large number of strong covalent bonds between carbon atoms must be broken.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning silicon is correct?

options.text: It consists of delocalized electrons and silicon cations.
It consists of cations and anions.

hint: Silicon is a semi-metal with a giant covalent structure. It conducts electricity at elevated temperatures.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning graphite is correct?

options.text: Intermolecular forces are present within a layer of graphite.
It is a good conductor of electricity.

hint: (A): Graphite consists of one element only. It is not a compound. (B): The carbon atoms are held together by strong covalent bonds within a layer. (C): Graphite is soft as the layers of carbon atoms are held together by weak van der Waals’ forces. (D): Graphite is a good conductor of electricity because there are delocalized electrons within the layers.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning graphite and diamond is correct?

options.text: They have the same relative atomic mass.
They have the same electrical conductivity.
They have the same boiling point and melting point.

hint: (B): Diamond is hard as it has a network of strong covalent bonds in its structure. However, graphite is soft as the layers of carbon atoms are held together by weak van der Waals’ forces. (C): Diamond cannot conduct electricity as there are no delocalized electrons in its structure. However, graphite can conduct electricity as it contains delocalized electrons which are free to move within the layers.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning substances with a giant covalent structure are correct? (1)All of them are hard. (2)All of them are insoluble in any solvents. (3)All of them have high melting points and boiling points.
hint: Graphite has a giant covalent structure. However, it is soft.

stem: Substances with a giant covalent structure are usually hard because

options.text: there are strong ionic bonds within the structure.
there are strong covalent bonds joining the atoms throughout the giant network.
there is strong attraction between the ions and the delocalized electrons.
there are strong intermolecular forces between the molecules.

hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning covalent compounds must be correct? (1)The atoms in covalent compounds are held together by covalent bonds. (2)All covalent compounds consist of discrete molecules. (3)All covalent compounds are non-electrolytes.
hint: Covalent compounds with a giant covalent structure do not consist of discrete molecules. Some covalent compounds (e.g. HCl and NH3) are electrolytes.

stem: Which of the following statements about dry ice and silicon dioxide is correct?

options.text: Both of them have simple molecular structures.
Both of them have high melting points.
Both of them are covalent compounds.
Both of them are soluble in water.

hint: CO2 has a simple molecular structure while SiO2 has a giant covalent structure. CO2 has a low melting point and it is slightly soluble in water. However, SiO2 is insoluble in water.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning silicon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide are correct? (1)Their constituent atoms are joined together by covalent bonds. (2)They have the same physical state at room temperature and pressure. (3)They do not conduct electricity in the liquid state.
hint: At room temperature and pressure, SiO2 is a solid while NO2 is a gas.

stem: Which of the following combinations correctly indicates the type of constituent particles in the substance under room conditions? Substance Constituent particles
hint: (A): Hydrogen exists as H2 molecules. (C): SiO2 has a giant covalent structure. No molecules can be found in SiO2. (D): Iron consists of iron(II) ions and delocalized electrons.

stem: Which of the following combinations is/are correct? Substance Attraction between particles (1) Iodine Intermolecular forces (2) Silicon dioxide Ionic bond (3) Calcium chloride Covalent bond
hint: (2): Silicon and oxygen atoms are joined together by covalent bonds. (3): Calcium ions and chloride ions are held together by ionic bonds.

stem: Which of the following combinations about the attraction between the particles in the following substances are correct? Substance Attraction between particles (1) Silicon Covalent bond (2) Lithium oxide Metallic bond (3) Argon Van der Waals’ forces
hint: The ions in lithium oxide are held together by ionic bonds.

stem: Which of the following combinations is INCORRECT? Substance Attraction between particles

options.text: Carbon dioxide Van der Waals’ forces

hint: The attraction between carbon atoms in diamond is covalent bond. There is no giant covalent bond although diamond has a giant covalent structure.

stem: Which of the following substances contains mobile ions?
hint: Review your notes.

stem: Which of the following solids consists of discrete molecules?
hint: Dry ice consists of carbon dioxide molecules.

stem: The table below shows the electrical conductivity of three substances. Substance Electrical conductivity X Conducts only in aqueous solution Y Conducts when molten or in aqueous solution Z Conducts when molten or in the solid state Which of the following combinations is correct? X Y Z
hint: H2SO4 is a covalent compound. When it dissolves in water, it ionizes to give mobile ions and so its aqueous solution can conduct electricity. NaBr is an ionic compound. Its ions become mobile when molten or in aqueous solution. Pb is a metal.

stem: Which of the following statements concerning iron and iron(III) oxide is correct?

options.text: They have a giant metallic structure.
They conduct electricity in the liquid state.

hint: (A): Iron has a giant metallic structure while iron(III) oxide has a giant ionic structure. (B): Liquid iron has delocalized electrons for conducting electricity while liquid iron(III) oxide has mobile ions for conducting electricity. (C): Iron is not an electrolyte but iron(III) oxide is. (D): Iron is a metal which is malleable but iron(III) oxide is not.

stem: Which of the following substances CANNOT conduct electricity?

options.text: An aqueous solution of calcium chloride

hint: Sugar has a simple molecular structure. It does not contain mobile ions or delocalized electrons in aqueous solution.

stem: Which of the following substances can conduct electricity in the molten state? (1)Sulphur (2)Anhydrous copper(II) sulphate (3)Gold
hint: Sulphur has a simple molecular structure. It does not contain mobile ions or delocalized electrons in the molten state.

stem: Which of the following substances has the lowest electrical conductivity?
hint: CH3OH has a simple molecular structure. It does not contain any mobile ions or delocalized electrons to conduct electricity. HNO3(aq) contains hydrogen ions and nitrate ions.

stem: Which of the following groups includes substances with different types of structures?

options.text: Carbon dioxide, silicon dioxide and sulphur dioxide
Propanoic acid, methanol and methyl propanoate

hint: CO2 and SO2 have simple molecular structures. SiO2 has a giant covalent structure.

stem: Which of the following substances contains particles that are the furthest apart from each other under room conditions?
hint: Among the four substances, only carbon dioxide is a gas under room conditions.

stem: Which of the following substances conducts electricity in its solid and liquid states?
hint: Silver is a metal which conducts electricity in its solid and liquid states.

stem: Which of the following correctly arranges three compounds: hydrogen chloride, sodium chloride and tetrachloromethane, in increasing order of melting point?
hint: Both HCl and CCl4 have simple molecular structures. As CCl4 has a larger molecular size than HCl, the melting point of CCl4 is higher. NaCl has a giant ionic structure. The ions are held together by strong ionic bonds and so it has the highest melting point.

stem: Which of the following combinations about the structures of substances is INCORRECT? Substance Structure
hint: N2H4 and SiCl4 exist as simple molecules. Sodium amalgam (Na/Hg) is an alloy of sodium and mercury. It has a giant metallic structure. K3Fe(CN)6 consists of K+ ions and Fe(CN)63 ions.

stem: Which of the following combinations about the descriptions of crystals is INCORRECT? Crystal Particles in the lattice Attraction between particles

options.text: Dry ice Molecules Van der Waals’ forces
Iodine Molecules Van der Waals’ forces

hint: In silver, the silver ions and delocalized electrons are held together by metallic bonds.

stem: The electron diagrams of an atom of P and an atom of Q are shown below. P and Q combine to form compound R. Which of the following statements concerning R are correct? (1)R has a giant covalent structure. (2)The formula of R is PQ3. (3)R cannot conduct electricity in the solid state.
hint: An atom of P shares electrons with three atoms of Q to form a covalent compound with a formula of PQ3. R has a simple molecular structure.

stem: The atomic numbers of elements X and Y are 6 and 8 respectively. The compound formed between X and Y (1)has a simple molecular structure. (2)is a gas under room conditions. (3)is a non-conductor of electricity.
hint: X and Y are carbon and oxygen respectively. The compound formed is carbon dioxide.

stem: A compound forms from element X (atomic number = 20) and element Y (atomic number = 35). Which of the following statements concerning the compound are correct? (1)It gives a brick-red flame in a flame test. (2)It conducts electricity in aqueous solution. (3)It dissolves in water to give a colourless solution.
hint: The compound is calcium bromide. Calcium compounds give a brick-red flame in a flame test. The ions in aqueous solution of calcium bromide are mobile and so it can conduct electricity.

stem: The electronic arrangements of atoms X and Y are 2, 8, 1 and 2, 8, 7 respectively. Which of the following predictions concerning the compound formed between X and Y is INCORRECT?

options.text: It has a high melting point.
It conducts electricity in the molten state.

hint: The compound formed is sodium chloride, which is soluble in water.

stem: Atoms of elements Q and R have 4 and 7 outermost shell electrons respectively. Which of the following statements concerning the compound formed between Q and R is correct?

options.text: It has a very high melting point.
It has a chemical formula of QR4.

hint: Q and R are non-metals. An atom of Q shares electrons with four atoms of R to form a covalent compound with a chemical formula of QR4.

stem: The electronic arrangements of the atoms of elements A and B are 2, 8, 8, 1 and 2, 8, 6 respectively. The compound formed between A and B

options.text: is a liquid under room conditions.

hint: A and B are potassium and sulphur respectively. The compound formed is potassium sulphide (K2S), which is an ionic compound.

stem: The atomic numbers of elements A and B are 11 and 16 respectively. The compound formed between A and B

options.text: is a gas at room temperature.

hint: The compound formed is an ionic compound (sodium sulphide). It should have a high melting point and boiling point. The compound (sodium sulphide) is soluble in water.

stem: Strontium is just below calcium in the Periodic Table. Which of the following statements concerning the chloride of strontium is INCORRECT?

options.text: It has a high melting point.
It is a good electrical conductor at room temperature.

hint: Strontium chloride is an ionic compound with a giant ionic structure. The ions in strontium chloride solid are not mobile. It cannot conduct electricity at room temperature.

stem: X, Y, and Z are three consecutive elements in the Periodic Table. The following table shows the information about the products of the reactions of the elements with iodine. Element Chemical formula of the product Structure of the product X IX Simple molecular structure Y No product forms / Z ZI Giant ionic structure Which of the following statements concerning X, Y and Z is correct?

options.text: X, Y and Z are in the same period of the Periodic Table.
X exists as monoatomic molecules under room conditions.
Y is a coloured substance under room conditions.

hint: (A): X, Y and Z are Group VII, Group 0 and Group I elements respectively. Among them, X and Y belong to the same period. (B): X (a halogen) exists as diatomic molecules under room conditions. (C): Y (a noble gas) is colourless.

stem: P, Q, R and S are among the first 20 elements in the Periodic Table. Their relative positions in the Periodic Table are shown as follows. P Q R S It is known that S exists as monoatomic molecules under room conditions. Which of the following statements concerning these elements and their compounds are correct? (1)All these elements are non-metals. (2)P and S do not react even at a high temperature. (3)The compound formed between Q and R has a simple molecular structure.
hint: From the given information, S should be argon and so P, Q and R should be fluorine, sulphur and chlorine respectively. Argon is unreactive and so it does not react with other elements. The compound of sulphur and chlorine (SCl2) has a simple molecular structure.

stem: Graphene is a single layer of graphite. A part of the structure of graphene is shown below. Each black dot represents a carbon atom. Which of the following statements concerning graphene is/are correct? (1)It is soluble in water. (2)It is a strong material. (3)It conducts electricity due to the presence of delocalized electrons.
hint: The carbon atoms in graphene are joined together by covalent bonds. Hence, it is difficult to separate the atoms.

stem: The following table shows the properties of four substances. Substance Physical state at room temperature Solubility in water A Solid Soluble B Solid Insoluble C Gas Insoluble D Liquid Insoluble Which of the following substances probably have a giant covalent structure?
hint: Substances with a giant covalent structure are all solids at room temperature and are insoluble in water.

stem: X is a non-conductor of electricity. It melts at 3C and boils at 44C. Which of the following statements concerning X must be correct? (1)X is a solid at room temperature. (2)The particles in X are held together by weak intermolecular forces. (3)X has a giant covalent structure.
hint: X has a simple molecular structure. It is a liquid at room temperature.

stem: If Y has a high melting point and conducts electricity when molten or in aqueous solution, Y probably has a
hint: Only ionic compounds conduct electricity when molten or in aqueous solution.

stem: Substance Y has a high melting point. It conducts electricity when molten and at the same time is decomposed by electricity. Y is likely to be
hint: Magnesium bromide is an ionic compound. It has a high melting point. Molten magnesium bromide undergoes electrolysis to give magnesium and bromine.

stem: The following table shows the properties of four substances. Substance Physical state under room conditions Does it conduct electricity? Solid state Molten state A Solid No Yes B Solid Yes Yes C Solid No No D Liquid Yes Which of the following substances probably have a giant ionic structure?
hint: Ionic compounds conduct electricity when molten or in aqueous solution.

stem: Consider the information listed in the table below. Substance Melting point / C Electrical conductivity W 1610 Poor in the solid state X 872 Good when in aqueous solution Y 45 Poor in any states Z 180 Good in the solid and liquid states Which of the following substances would probably have a giant covalent structure?
hint: Substances with a giant covalent structure have very high melting points and cannot conduct electricity (except graphite).

stem: X, Y and Z are three elements and the properties of their chlorides are given below. Melting point / C Electrical conductivity in the molten state Chloride of X 4 Poor Chloride of Y 873 Good Chloride of Z 677 Poor Which of the following statements must be correct?

options.text: The chloride of X is a liquid at room temperature.
The chlorides of Y and Z have the same type of structure.
The chloride of Y conducts electricity in aqueous solution.

hint: (A): The physical state of the chloride of X not only depends on its melting point, but also its boiling point. If its boiling point is lower than room temperature, it is a gas at room temperature. (B): Since the chlorides of Y and Z have different electrical conductivities in the molten state, they probably have different types of structure. (C): If the chloride of Y is insoluble in water, the mixture of the chloride of Y and water does not conduct electricity. (D): Since the chloride of X has a low melting point, it probably has a simple molecular structure. It may be a compound of a non-metal (or a semi-metal) and chlorine.

stem: The melting points of some chlorides are given in the table below. Chloride NaCl SiCl4 PCl3 Melting point / C 801 70 92 Which of the following can be deduced from the above data? (1)NaCl cannot conduct electricity at 800C. (2)SiCl4 is a covalent compound with a simple molecular structure. (3)PCl3 is a covalent compound with a giant covalent structure.
hint: NaCl is a solid at 800C. It cannot conduct electricity in the solid state. PCl3 should have a simple molecular structure as it has a low melting point.

stem: The table below shows the melting points of two chlorides. Melting point / C Chloride of X 50 Chloride of Y 1000 Which of the following statements is correct?

options.text: Both X and Y are metals.
The chloride of X can conduct electricity in the molten state.
The chloride of X has a giant covalent structure.
The chloride of Y can conduct electricity at 1000C.

hint: The low melting point of the chloride of X indicates that it has a simple molecular structure. X should be a non-metal forming a covalent compound with chlorine. No ions or delocalized electrons are present in the chloride of X. The chloride of Y should be an ionic compound and the ions become mobile when it melts.

stem: The melting point and boiling point of J are 801C and 1465C respectively. In the molten state, J conducts electricity with decomposition. J probably has
hint: J decomposes in its molten state under electricity. Hence, it is an ionic compound.

stem: A compound is a solid with a chemical formula XY2. It has a high melting point and conducts electricity in aqueous solution. Which of the following may the compound be?
hint: The formula of sodium fluoride and potassium sulphide are NaF and K2S respectively. Silicon dioxide (SiO2) is insoluble in water.

stem: A substance is soluble in water and does not conduct electricity in the solid state. The substance could be (1)an element with a giant covalent structure. (2)a compound with a simple molecular structure. (3)a compound with a giant ionic structure.
hint: Substances with a giant covalent structure are insoluble in any solvents.

stem: Consider the following information about four substances P, Q, R and S. Substance Melting point / C Electrical conductivity at room temperature P 7 Poor Q 39 Good R 801 Poor S 660 Good Which of the following substances has a giant metallic structure and is a solid at room temperature?
hint: Substances with a giant metallic structure can conduct of electricity at room temperature. Q is not a solid at room temperature.

stem: The table below gives some information on four substances. Substance Melting point / C Electrical conductivity in the solid state Electrical conductivity in the liquid state Solubility in water W 17 Poor Poor Soluble X 455 Poor Good Soluble Y 1064 Good Good Insoluble Z 3550 Poor Poor Insoluble Which of the following deductions is correct?

options.text: W has a simple molecular structure.
X is a solid metal at room temperature and pressure.
The particles in Y are held together by intermolecular forces.
Z consists of oppositely charged ions.

hint: (B): X is not a metal as it is a poor conductor of electricity in the solid state. (C): Y should have a giant metallic structure. There are no discrete molecules in Y. (D): Z is not an ionic compound. Ionic compounds can conduct electricity in the liquid state.

stem: The table below shows the electrical conductivities of four substances W, X, Y and Z at different states. In the table, ‘’ and ‘’ represent ‘can conduct electricity’ and ‘cannot conduct electricity’ respectively. Substance Solid state Liquid state Aqueous solution W Insoluble in water X Insoluble in water Y Z Which of the following substances is likely to be wax?
hint: Wax has a simple molecular structure. It does not contain mobile ions in the solid or liquid states and it is insoluble in water.

stem: Boron nitride is a compound of boron and nitrogen. The structure and bonding of a type of boron nitride, hexagonal boron nitride, are similar to those of graphite. The structure of hexagonal boron nitride is shown below. BNBN B N B N Which of the following statements concerning hexagonal boron nitride are correct? (1)The boron atoms and the nitrogen atoms are held together by covalent bonds. (2)Van der Waals’ forces are present in the structure of hexagonal boron nitride. (3)It can be used as a lubricant.
hint: Van der Waals’ forces are present between the layers of atoms in the structure of hexagonal boron nitride.

stem: Boron and nitrogen react to form boron nitride that has a layered structure similar to that of graphite. Which of the following statements concerning boron nitride are correct? (1)It has a high melting point. (2)It can be used as a lubricant. (3)It is insoluble in water.
hint: Boron nitride, like graphite, has a giant covalent structure. The layers are held together by weak van der Waals’ forces and they can easily slide over each other.

stem: Cubic boron nitride is widely used as an abrasive. The diagram below shows the arrangement of atoms in the structure of cubic boron nitride. BNBN B N B N Which of the following statements concerning cubic boron nitride is/are correct? (1)It is a hard material. (2)It is a brittle material. (3)It is an electrical conductor.
hint: The structure of cubic boron nitride is similar to that of diamond. The boron and nitrogen atoms are held together by strong covalent bonds and so it is hard. It cannot conduct electricity as there are no delocalized electrons in its structure.
